name: Shorley
overview: Shorley is a very small rural hamlet in Cheriton parish in the City of Winchester district of Hampshire, lying about a mile south-east of Cheriton village near New Cheriton and Beauworth, within the South Downs National Park. Historically the south-east corner of Cheriton parish, it is known for Shorley Copse woodland and the former Shorley Pottery Works. It is a scattered settlement of houses and farms with no amenities of its own; the nearest village facilities are in Cheriton, with fuller shops and services in New Alresford (~4 miles) and Winchester (~8 miles).
character: A secluded, wooded downland hamlet of houses and farmsteads set among copses and chalk countryside in the South Downs National Park. The feel is very rural and private, with extensive walking and the quiet, well-regarded village of Cheriton close by.
amenities: No shop or pub in Shorley itself
Two pubs (including the Flower Pots Inn with its own microbrewery), a shop/post office and village hall in Cheriton (~1 mi)
Cheriton primary school, cricket pitch and tennis courts
Full shopping and services in Winchester (~8 mi)

commutes.notes: Nearest town; Watercress Line heritage railway and amenities
Via the A272/B3046; mainline station to London Waterloo
Via the A272; mainline to London Waterloo (Portsmouth line)
From Winchester station (SWR), drive ~20 min to the station

prices.summary: Shorley and the Cheriton area are a premium South Downs location; housing is dominated by detached, period and listed country houses, with recent sales in the area frequently £700k-£1m+ and substantial houses well above. Supply is scarce.

thingsToDo: Walking the South Downs National Park footpaths and Shorley Copse
Hinton Ampner gardens and house (National Trust, nearby)
The Flower Pots Inn and microbrewery in Cheriton
Watercress Line heritage railway at New Alresford
placesToEat: The Flower Pots Inn, Cheriton (~1 mi)
Other Cheriton village pub and nearby Hinton Arms (Bramdean)
Wide choice of pubs and cafes in New Alresford (~4 mi)
pros: Very quiet, wooded setting inside the South Downs National Park
Good village primary and two pubs in Cheriton nearby
Within reach of Alresford and Winchester's mainline
Outstanding walking and countryside on the doorstep
cons: No amenities in the hamlet itself; car essential
Expensive and very limited housing stock
National Park planning constraints on alterations
No mainline station within walking distance
whoItSuits: Buyers seeking a secluded, high-value hamlet in the South Downs near Cheriton, with good village schooling and Alresford/Winchester within easy reach, who can match the premium country-house market.
nearestStation: Winchester (~8 miles, SWR mainline); Alresford (~4 miles, heritage line)
